findings.location: design.md §3 (logging 'structured logs to file'); requirements.md FR-8/AC-1 ('rotating logs'); CLAUDE.md ('tracing for logs'); tasks.md 0.x/2.3 (logs via tracing)
findings.issue: `tracing` writes structured logs to a file, and the ingest path carries the raw `tool_input.command` and the full statusline JSON (paths, cost, model). Nothing in the spec forbids logging these payloads verbatim, and no log-file permission is specified. A debug/info log line that prints the ingest event would persist secrets/prompts/paths to a (potentially 0644) log file — re-introducing the very leak the emit path tries to prevent, just to disk instead of Discord. NFR-3 only governs egress, not local logs.
findings.evidence: design.md:49 'structured logs to file'; requirements.md:123 'rotating logs'; design.md §4.1 hook event example contains `"tool_input":{"command":"cargo check"}` and statusline contains cwd — these are exactly the structs that would be `tracing::debug!(?event)`-logged; research-dossier investigations[2].risks 'never surface … only derive counts/status, never display task descriptions verbatim'. No spec line restricts log content or sets log perms.
findings.fix: Add an AC under NFR-3 / FR-8: the daemon MUST NOT log raw `tool_input`, raw prompt/transcript text, full paths, or unredacted statusline JSON at any level; only log redacted/structured summaries. Set the log file mode to 0600 and place it under a 0700 state dir. Add a privacy.rs unit test (tasks 5.1) that the log formatter for IngestEvent omits the command/paths.

findings.location: design.md §4.1 (socket at ~/.local/state/claude-presence/daemon.sock); tasks.md 0.2 (config at ~/.config/claude-presence/config.toml), 3.1 (socket server), 4.1 (launchd/log paths)
findings.issue: No permission requirement on the daemon ingest socket, the config file, the state dir, or the log dir. The ingest socket accepts activity-control JSON; if world-writable, any local process could spoof presence (integrity), and if the config/log/state are world-readable they expose blacklist contents and any logged data. Live check shows the parent dirs are NOT private by default: ~/.config and ~/.local/state are drwxr-xr-x (0755). The dossier explicitly raised this (A3 risk #3: 'Several files are 0600/0700 … the app cannot be a shared/root daemon without permission handling').
findings.evidence: design.md:93 'Unix socket at ~/.local/state/claude-presence/daemon.sock'; tasks.md:23 '~/.config/claude-presence/config.toml'; research-dossier investigations[2].risks[3] '0600/0700 … permission handling'. Live: `ls -lad ~/.config ~/.local/state` → both `drwxr-xr-x` (0755); contrast ~/.claude/sessions is `drwx------`. So nothing makes our dirs private unless the spec mandates it.
findings.fix: Add to FR-7/FR-8: create the state dir 0700 and the config dir 0700; chmod the daemon.sock to 0600 (or 0700) and bind it inside the 0700 state dir; write config.toml and log files 0600; the ingest server SHOULD verify the peer uid == own uid (SO_PEERCRED/getpeereid) and reject others. Reference these in tasks 3.1 (socket) and 4.1 (paths/launchd).

findings.location: requirements.md:21 (buttons 'project / about links'); design.md §4.3 buttons row ('from git remote / config') and §3 platform/macos.rs
findings.issue: Buttons can leak the full local path and private repo URL. The dossier's mapping is `buttons[0] = file:///Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private` (full home path) and `buttons[1] = <private PR URL>`. The spec sources buttons 'from git remote / config' with no requirement to (a) omit/redact them by default or (b) respect the blacklist. (Separately, Discord requires https:// button URLs, so a `file://` button is also technically invalid — but the privacy point stands regardless.)
findings.evidence: requirements.md:21 'optional buttons: project / about links'; design.md:134 'optional; from git remote / config'; research-dossier investigations[1].rp_field_mappings buttons[0] `file:///Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private`, buttons[1] private PR URL; investigations[9].rp_field_mappings notes 'Avoid leaking private repo URLs by default.'
findings.fix: Add an AC to FR-7/AC-2: buttons are OFF by default and opt-in per repo; never emit a `file://` path or a private-repo remote URL; when emitted, the URL host/path must pass the blacklist and be https-only. Note in design §4.3 that the git-remote button is suppressed for blacklisted repos and for any non-public remote.

findings.location: requirements.md FR-7/AC-2 + FR-5/AC-3; design.md §4.3 details/state rows
findings.issue: cwd/project-slug and branch names are themselves emitted (project + branch are in `details`), and these can be sensitive (e.g. a branch named after an unreleased product or a client). FR-7/AC-2 covers 'blacklisted projects' but the spec does not say the blacklist matches the project NAME/branch (not just the full path), nor that a non-blacklisted-but-still-revealing slug is handled. CLAUDE.md says 'full paths (basename only)', which still emits the project basename and branch verbatim.
findings.evidence: design.md:128 details = '{verb} {target} — {project} ({branch})'; requirements.md:118 'repo/path blacklist (blacklisted projects show a generic label or nothing)'; tasks.md 0.2 config key is `blacklist_paths` (paths only). research-dossier investigations[9].risks 'details/state lines … must be sanitized — show project slug + branch … never raw prompt content or full file paths' — acknowledges slug/branch are the chosen surface but assumes the user accepts that.
findings.fix: Clarify in FR-7/AC-2 that the blacklist matches on project name, branch, AND path (rename `blacklist_paths`→`blacklist` or add `blacklist_repos`/`blacklist_branches`), and that 'private mode' (tasks 5.1) replaces project+branch with a generic label. Add a test that a blacklisted branch/project never reaches details/state.

findings.location: design.md §4.1 ('statusline event (already-sanitized subset)'); tasks.md 3.2 (statusline-wrapper.sh) and 3.1 (ingest)
findings.issue: The design ASSERTS the statusline event is an 'already-sanitized subset', but the sanitization is supposed to happen in the bundled `claude-presence forward` step / wrapper, and no task makes that subsetting/redaction explicit. The raw CC statusline JSON includes `current_dir`, `project_dir`, `original_cwd`, `git_worktree`, `cost`, etc. If the wrapper tees the full JSON to the socket (the natural shell implementation), the 'already-sanitized' claim is false and full paths reach the daemon (and risk being logged).
findings.evidence: design.md:104 comment 'already-sanitized subset of CC's statusline JSON'; design.md:85 'statusline-wrapper.sh … tees stdin JSON to the daemon socket'; tasks.md:115 'tees the JSON to claude-presence forward --kind statusline'. Live CLI binary confirms the statusline JSON exposes full paths: strings show `"current_dir": "string"  // Current working directory path`, `"project_dir"`, `"original_cwd"`, `"git_worktree"`.
findings.fix: Make the subsetting a real, located step: specify in tasks 3.1/3.2 that `claude-presence forward --kind statusline` parses CC's JSON and emits ONLY the whitelisted fields (model display_name, effort.level, cost.total_cost_usd, context_window.used_percentage/context_window_size, version) — basename-only for any dir — and never forwards current_dir/project_dir/original_cwd raw. Update the §4.1 comment to point at that code path.

missing_items: A requirement that the Bash (and any tool) command/argument input is secret-scrubbed (token/key/password/Authorization/URL-credential/env-assignment patterns) before it can appear in any public Discord field — currently only paths→basename + blacklist are required.
A requirement forbidding the daemon from logging raw payloads (tool_input.command, prompt/transcript text, full paths, unredacted statusline JSON) via tracing, plus log-file mode 0600.
Explicit permission requirements: state dir 0700, config dir 0700, config.toml 0600, daemon.sock 0600 + peer-uid check (SO_PEERCRED/getpeereid) on the ingest socket.
A policy for ai-title: off by default, redaction-gated, blacklist-aware (currently extracted with no emission gate).
Buttons privacy policy: opt-in, https-only, no file:// paths, no private-repo URLs, blacklist-aware.
Clarification that the repo/path blacklist matches project name and branch, not only the absolute path (config key is `blacklist_paths`).
An explicit, located statusline whitelisting/subsetting step (which fields the forward subcommand keeps) so the design's 'already-sanitized subset' claim is actually enforced and tested.

reviews.dimension: R8 — Internal consistency across requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md, CLAUDE.md
reviews.summary: The four docs are largely consistent: numbers (128-char caps, 1M/200k context windows, 2.5s/15s intervals, ~5/20s rate limit), the verify gate (cargo fmt/clippy/test), version 2.1.181, hook event names, and all FR-1..9 ACs are cited by at least one task and trace cleanly. Cross-checking against the live CLI bundle confirmed every Anthropic identifier the docs depend on (SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/SubagentStart/SubagentStop, total_cost_usd, used_percentage, context_window_size, display_name, isSidechain, gitBranch, sessionId) actually exists in claude v2.1.181. The defects are localized terminology/coverage mismatches, the most notable being: (1) the `forward` subcommand exists in design ADR-9 and all of tasks but is absent from requirements FR-8/AC-2 even though task 0.1 cites FR-8/AC-2 as its AC; (2) design's architecture diagram names a `collectors::` module that does not exist in the §3 layout or tasks (which use claude/ + ingest/); (3) party.size[0] is described as the running count in one place and the busy count in three others. None are build-blocking, but several would confuse an implementing agent or produce a card that disagrees with the requirements example.

findings.location: requirements.md FR-8/AC-2 (line 124) vs design.md §2 (line 48), §4.1/ADR-9 (lines 97,160), tasks.md 0.1 (line 13) & 3.1 (line 107)
findings.issue: The `forward` CLI subcommand is required by design (ADR-9: scripts pipe into `claude-presence forward --kind hook|statusline`) and is wired/implemented across tasks 0.1, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3 — but it is ABSENT from requirements FR-8/AC-2, which enumerates only `run`, `install`, `uninstall`, `status`, `doctor`. design §2's clap row (line 48) also omits `forward`, listing only `run/install/uninstall/status/doctor`. Yet task 0.1 cites `**AC**: FR-8/AC-2` for the skeleton that includes `forward`. So the AC the task points to does not actually authorize the `forward` subcommand.
findings.evidence: requirements.md:124 `**AC-2** CLI subcommands: \`run\` (foreground), \`install\` ... \`uninstall\` ... \`status\`, \`doctor\`` (no forward). design.md:48 `| CLI | \`clap\` (derive) | \`run\`/\`install\`/\`uninstall\`/\`status\`/\`doctor\` |` (no forward). tasks.md:13 `wire \`clap\` subcommands \`run | install | uninstall | status | doctor | forward\``; tasks.md:16 `- **AC**: FR-8/AC-2`. design.md:97 `scripts pipe their stdin into \`claude-presence forward --kind hook|statusline\``.
findings.fix: Add `forward` to requirements.md FR-8/AC-2's subcommand list (e.g. "...`doctor`, and an internal `forward` (used by the chained hook/statusline scripts to pipe events to the daemon socket; not user-facing)"), and add `forward` to the design.md §2 CLI table row so all three docs agree.

findings.location: design.md §1 architecture diagram (lines 13-15) vs design.md §3 module layout (lines 64-72) vs tasks.md Files
findings.issue: The §1 diagram labels the three input collectors `collectors::sessions`, `collectors::transcript`, `collectors::ingest`, implying a `src/collectors/` module. But the §3 module layout (and every tasks.md Files entry) places these at `src/claude/sessions.rs`, `src/claude/transcript.rs`, and `src/ingest/socket.rs` — there is no `collectors/` directory anywhere. An implementing agent reading the diagram could create the wrong module path.
findings.evidence: design.md:13 `collectors::sessions`, design.md:14 `collectors::transcript`, design.md:15 `collectors::ingest`; but design.md:66 `sessions.rs` (under `claude/`), design.md:67 `transcript.rs` (under `claude/`), design.md:71 `socket.rs` (under `ingest/`). `grep -rn 'src/collectors' specs/` returns nothing. tasks.md:56 Files `src/claude/sessions.rs`; tasks.md:75 `src/claude/transcript.rs`; tasks.md:110 `src/ingest/socket.rs`.
findings.fix: In design.md §1 relabel the diagram nodes to match the real module paths: `claude::sessions`, `claude::transcript`, `ingest::socket` (or annotate that `collectors::` is a conceptual grouping, not a module path). Keep §3/tasks as the source of truth.

findings.location: requirements.md line 19 vs requirements.md FR-5/AC-2 (line 99) vs design.md §4.3 (line 131) vs tasks.md 2.1 (line 81)
findings.issue: party.size[0] semantics disagree. Requirements line 19 defines `party.size [current, max]` as "how many sessions/agents are RUNNING" with example "(2 of 5)" — first element = running/live count. But requirements FR-5/AC-2, design §4.3, and tasks 2.1 all set the first element to the BUSY count (`[busy_count, total_live_count]` / `[busy_count, max|total_count]` / `[busy, total/capacity]`). Running count and busy count are different quantities (e.g. 4 sessions live but 2 busy), so the rendered "(N of M)" will not match the line-19 description.
findings.evidence: requirements.md:19 `**party.size** \`[current, max]\`: how many sessions/agents are running (renders "(2 of 5)")`. requirements.md:99 `**AC-2** \`party.size = [busy_count, total_live_count]\``. design.md:131 `| \`party.size\` | \`[busy_count, max]\` or \`[busy_count, total_count]\` |`. tasks.md:81 `\`party.size=[busy,total/capacity]\``.
findings.fix: Reconcile to one definition. Either change requirements.md:19 to `[busy_count, max]` with "how many sessions are actively working (busy) of capacity", or change FR-5/AC-2 + design + tasks to use the live/running count as the first element. Recommend the former (busy_count) since three of four docs already use it.

findings.location: design.md §3 statusline-wrapper.sh (line 85) vs requirements.md FR-3/AC-1 (lines 71-72) vs tasks.md 3.2 (lines 114-117)
findings.issue: The statusline-wrapper's mechanism for obtaining the user's original command is described two incompatible ways. design §3 says the wrapper `reads $CC_PRESENCE_INNER` (an env var that appears in NO other doc). requirements FR-3/AC-1 and tasks 3.2 say the install step `reads the user's current statusLine.command from settings` and `store[s] it` / the wrapper `runs it`. Whether the inner command is injected via `$CC_PRESENCE_INNER` at runtime or baked into the installed wrapper at install time is left contradictory/underspecified.
findings.evidence: design.md:85 `statusline-wrapper.sh   reads $CC_PRESENCE_INNER, runs it, tees stdin JSON to the daemon socket`. requirements.md:71-72 `Install a statusline-wrapper that reads the user's current \`statusLine.command\` from settings, invokes it ...`. tasks.md:115 `install the wrapper that runs it (stdout passthrough) and tees the JSON`. `grep CC_PRESENCE_INNER` hits only design.md.
findings.fix: Pick one mechanism and state it in all three docs. If using `$CC_PRESENCE_INNER`, define it in requirements FR-3/AC-1 and tasks 3.2 (and how install sets it). If baking the stored command into the wrapper at install time, remove the `$CC_PRESENCE_INNER` reference from design.md:85.

findings.location: CLAUDE.md line 14 (Conventions) vs design.md §2 deps table (lines 40-51) vs tasks.md 0.1 dep list (lines 14-15)
findings.issue: CLAUDE.md mandates `thiserror` for errors ("Prefer `thiserror` for errors"), but `thiserror` is absent from both the design §2 dependency table and the tasks 0.1 explicit dependency list. An agent scaffolding Cargo.toml from task 0.1 would not add it, contradicting the project convention.
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:14 `Prefer \`thiserror\` for errors`. design.md:40-51 deps table (no thiserror row). tasks.md:14-15 `Add deps from design §2 (tokio, notify, sysinfo, libproc, nix, serde, serde_json, discord-rich-presence = "1.1", toml, clap, tracing, tracing-subscriber, directories).` (no thiserror). `grep thiserror` hits only CLAUDE.md.
findings.fix: Add a `thiserror` row to the design.md §2 table and include `thiserror` in the tasks.md 0.1 dependency list, so the convention is realized in the dependency graph.

findings.location: requirements.md line 101 (FR-5/AC-3) vs design.md §5 Truncation (lines 144-145) & §4.3 (line 129)
findings.issue: The truncation strategy is described with conflicting priority. Requirements FR-5/AC-3 says "abbreviate model/plan first, drop least-important metric last" — i.e. abbreviation (incl. plan) precedes any metric drop. Design §5 says "drop metrics from the tail (ctx% → tokens → cost → plan) and abbreviate model" — here metrics are dropped first and the drop ladder INCLUDES `plan` as a droppable metric, while model abbreviation is mentioned but plan abbreviation is not. So requirements treats plan as abbreviate-first; design treats plan as a droppable-last metric.
findings.evidence: requirements.md:101 `graceful truncation to ≤128 chars (abbreviate model/plan first, drop least-important metric last)`. design.md:144-145 `if >128, drop metrics from the tail (ctx% → tokens → cost → plan) and abbreviate model ("Opus 4.8"→"O4.8") until it fits`.
findings.fix: Align the algorithm: state a single ordered policy, e.g. "1) abbreviate model and plan; 2) if still >128, drop metrics tail-first: ctx% → tokens → cost". Decide whether `plan` is abbreviated (per requirements) or dropped (per design) and make both docs say the same thing.

findings.location: design.md §3 module layout (line 83) & §2 (line 51, tray) vs tasks.md Files
findings.issue: Two design features have no owning task / no module-layout slot. (a) design §3 platform/macos.rs lists a `frontmost-app (best-effort)` function, but the only task touching platform/macos.rs (task 1.3) covers exe-filter + lsof cwd fallback only — frontmost-app is never assigned to any task. (b) src/tray.rs (tasks 5.2) is NOT in the design §3 module layout even though §2 and requirements FR-9 cover the tray. Minor orphans, not contradictions.
findings.evidence: design.md:83 `platform/macos.rs  $TMPDIR discord sockets, frontmost-app (best-effort), lsof cwd fallback`; tasks.md:50-56 (task 1.3) describes macos.rs as exe-filter + libproc cwd only, no frontmost-app; `grep frontmost tasks.md` = none. tasks.md:160 Files `src/tray.rs`; design §3 layout (lines 60-83) has no tray.rs entry.
findings.fix: Either drop `frontmost-app (best-effort)` from design.md:83 (consistent with ADR-7 saying true focus is unobtainable) or add a task line for it. Add `tray.rs   (Phase 5) menu-bar control` to the design §3 module layout for symmetry with tasks 5.2.

findings.location: design.md §4.1 (lines 96-97)
findings.issue: Within a single paragraph the subcommand is first named `claude-presence ingest` (as a candidate) and then resolved to `claude-presence forward` by ADR-9. The leftover `ingest` mention could mislead a reader skimming for the subcommand name, since `ingest` is also the module directory name (collision).
findings.evidence: design.md:96 `use a bundled \`claude-presence ingest\` subcommand the scripts pipe into, or \`socat\` if present). Decision`; design.md:97 `(ADR-9): scripts pipe their stdin into \`claude-presence forward --kind hook|statusline\``.
findings.fix: Reword line 96 to not name a phantom `ingest` subcommand, e.g. "use a bundled subcommand the scripts pipe into, or `socat` if present)" — leaving `forward` (line 97) as the only named subcommand.

findings.location: tasks.md 0.2 (line 23, config path) vs requirements.md FR-7 / design.md §4.1 (line 93)
findings.issue: tasks.md 0.2 pins the config path to `~/.config/claude-presence/config.toml` while the daemon socket/state lives at `~/.local/state/claude-presence/daemon.sock` (design §4.1). The two XDG roots (.config vs .local/state) are individually correct but the split is never stated in requirements or design, and requirements FR-7/AC-1 names no path at all — so the only place the config path appears is one task. Low-risk but an undocumented divergence a reviewer might flag.
findings.evidence: tasks.md:23 `Load from \`~/.config/claude-presence/config.toml\` with built-in defaults`. design.md:93 `Unix socket at \`~/.local/state/claude-presence/daemon.sock\` (configurable)`. requirements.md FR-7 (lines 115-120) names no path.
findings.fix: State both well-known paths in one place (design §4.1 or install/paths.rs description): config at `~/.config/claude-presence/config.toml`, state/socket at `~/.local/state/claude-presence/`, so the XDG split is intentional and documented rather than appearing only in a single task.

strengths: All FR-1 through FR-9 acceptance criteria are cited by at least one task; the FR/AC traceability between requirements and tasks is complete and accurate (verified by grep: every FR-N/AC-M defined in requirements appears on a task AC line).

Numeric facts are consistent across all four docs: 128-char caps (requirements C-3 / FR-5/AC-3, design §4.3 & §5, tasks 2.1), 1M vs 200k context windows (requirements FR-3/AC-4, tasks 1.2, CLAUDE.md, design ingest example ctx_size:1000000), 2.5s/15s default intervals (design §5 = requirements' ~2-3s/~15s), and ~5/20s rate limit.
The verify gate matches exactly between CLAUDE.md line 9 and tasks.md line 3 (`cargo fmt --check` / `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` / `cargo test`).

Every Anthropic-stable identifier the spec depends on was confirmed to exist in the live CLI binary v2.1.181: all six hook events (incl. the less-common SubagentStart, 28 hits), and the statusLine/transcript fields total_cost_usd, used_percentage, context_window_size, display_name, total_duration_ms, isSidechain, gitBranch, sessionId.

All 9 ADRs in design map to a requirement/task (ADR-1→FR-3/ADR sources, ADR-2→FR-1/AC-1+task1.3, ADR-3→C-2/FR-5, ADR-4→C-6/tasks3.2-3.3, ADR-5→C-4/task1.1, ADR-6→task2.2, ADR-7→FR-5/AC-1, ADR-8→C-7/task5.1, ADR-9→task0.1+3.1).

camelCase (Claude's own JSON: sessionId, startedAt, gitBranch) vs snake_case (the project's own ingest JSON and Rust structs: session_id, started_at) is applied consistently and correctly at the data boundary — not an inconsistency.

The example presence strings agree: requirements' `Opus 4.8 (High) · Max · $0.98 · ... Ctx 8%` matches design's template and ingest example (cost_usd:0.98, ctx_pct:8.3 → 8%), and the cost worked-example numbers in task 1.2 (131/12570/59709/10493) are present in the research-dossier ground truth.
missing_items: Constraints C-1 (Rust/macOS-only, structure for later portability) and C-5 (no root/no bot token/no OAuth — local Discord IPC only) are never cited on any task's AC line, unlike C-2/C-3/C-4/C-6/C-7. C-5's IPC-only guarantee in particular has no explicit task acceptance hook even though FR-6 tasks implement it.

NFR-1 (performance/<10ms hook), NFR-2 (reliability/never slow CC), NFR-4 (maintainability/adapter), NFR-5 (portability) are not cited by any task; only NFR-3 and NFR-6 are referenced. The <10ms hook handler requirement (NFR-1, FR-4/AC-3) is cited via FR-4/AC-3 in task 3.3 but NFR-1 itself is untraced.
A single canonical definition of party.size[0] (running vs busy) — currently the docs carry two contradictory definitions and no doc declares which is authoritative.

Definition/ownership of the `$CC_PRESENCE_INNER` environment variable — it appears once (design §3) with no spec or task explaining who sets it or whether it is the chosen mechanism over the install-time settings read.

synthesis.headline: The specs are unusually well-grounded — every Anthropic/Discord identifier (hook events SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/SubagentStart/SubagentStop, statusLine fields total_cost_usd/used_percentage/context_window_size/display_name/total_duration_ms/exceeds_200k_tokens/effort, the 1M-window table, the exe-path filter) is confirmed present in the live v2.1.181 binary, and the chosen crate (discord-rich-presence 1.1.0) really exposes every field the card needs. They are NOT yet 100% buildable. Five issues will produce a wrong or broken build and must be edited first: (1) timestamps.start is documented as epoch SECONDS in design.md but both the pinned crate's own doc-comment and the official Discord docs say MILLISECONDS — a seconds value yields a ~1000x-wrong elapsed timer (the headline Codex-parity field); (2) the inherited code idiom DiscordIpcClient::new(id)? will not compile against 1.1.0 where new() returns Self, not Result; (3) the statusline-wrapper's $CC_PRESENCE_INNER env mechanism is impossible — Claude Code injects no such var (0 hits in the binary; statusLine setting shape is only {type,command}, data arrives on stdin), so the original command must be baked into the wrapper at install time; (4) tasks 2.3 and 3.1 both WRITE src/main.rs with no ordering dependency, so the parallel orchestrator can clobber one writer; (5) party.size is defined four inconsistent ways across the docs and using busy_count as element 0 risks an empty/degenerate counter. After these edits plus the should-fix set (missing plist keys, KeepAlive semantics, type-ownership inversion, secret-scrubbing of Bash commands, log/socket privacy), the specs are ready to build.

must_fix.location: design.md §4.3, table row `timestamps.start` (line 130); requirements.md FR-5/AC-4 (line 102) and Product goal `timestamps.start` (line 18)
must_fix.issue: timestamps.start unit is wrong. design.md states 'epoch secs; verified seconds accepted', but the pinned crate's OWN doc-comment (discord-rich-presence-1.1.0/src/activity.rs:60 `/// Unix time (in milliseconds) of when the activity started`) and the official Discord docs (docs.discord.food/resources/presence: 'Unix timestamps (in milliseconds)') both say MILLISECONDS. The dossier's 'seconds accepted' came from a live test that only confirmed Discord ECHOED the value, not that it RENDERED a correct timer — and the dossier itself flags this as confidence:medium with 'confirm the rendered timer is correct, not off by 1000x'. A seconds value parsed as ms places activity start near 1970, producing a grossly wrong elapsed timer.
must_fix.concrete_edit: In design.md §4.3 change the timestamps.start note from 'focused session start (epoch secs; verified seconds accepted)' to 'focused session start, epoch MILLISECONDS (started_at as ms since epoch); Discord renders the elapsed timer from this'. Add the conversion contract: started_at: SystemTime → Timestamps::new().start(dur.as_millis() as i64). In tasks.md 2.2 add an AC: the value handed to the crate is in milliseconds (13 digits for current dates). Remove the false 'verified seconds accepted' clause.
must_fix.raised_by: R2 (verified by me: crate src + official docs both say ms; dossier echo!=render)

must_fix.location: tasks.md 2.3 (Files: src/main.rs, Depends: 2.2, line 100) and 3.1 (Files include src/main.rs, Depends: 2.1, line 111)
must_fix.issue: Tasks 2.3 and 3.1 both WRITE src/main.rs but neither depends on the other (paths 2.1→2.2→2.3 and 2.1→3.1 never cross). The executing-plans orchestrator schedules a task the moment its declared Depends complete, so 3.1 becomes ready right after 2.1 — concurrently with the 2.2→2.3 chain. Two tasks editing src/main.rs in the same wave means the second writer clobbers the first's edits.
must_fix.concrete_edit: In tasks.md task 3.1 change `**Depends**: 2.1` to `**Depends**: 2.1, 2.3`. This serializes the two src/main.rs writers (3.1 wires the ingest server boot + `forward` subcommand into the `run` wiring 2.3 established) without adding any false logical dependency.

must_fix.location: design.md §3 assets block, line 85 (`statusline-wrapper.sh reads $CC_PRESENCE_INNER`); cross-refs requirements.md FR-3/AC-1 (lines 71-74) and tasks.md 3.2 (lines 113-117)
must_fix.issue: The wrapper mechanism as written cannot work and contradicts the other two docs. Claude Code injects NO $CC_PRESENCE_INNER (0 hits in the v2.1.181 binary; the only statusline env vars CC sets are COLUMNS/LINES per v2.1.153+, and session data arrives on stdin). The statusLine setting shape is only {type:command, command:<string>} with no env field — there is no way to inject a custom env var. design.md:85 also conflicts with FR-3/AC-1 and tasks 3.2, which say the installer reads and STORES the user's current statusLine.command.
must_fix.concrete_edit: Reword design.md:85 to match FR-3/AC-1 and tasks 3.2: at install, capture the original statusLine.command and bake it into the installed wrapper script (or read it from a fixed state-dir path); the wrapper execs that stored inner command, passes its stdout through unchanged, and tees the stdin JSON to `claude-presence forward --kind statusline`. Delete every reference to $CC_PRESENCE_INNER (it appears only in design.md).
must_fix.raised_by: R3, R6, R8 (verified: 0 binary hits for the var; statusline docs confirm stdin-only + {type,command}-only shape)

must_fix.location: design.md §2 (discord row, line 46) and §4.3; tasks.md 0.1 (line 15) and 2.2; cross-ref research-dossier C1/C2 sample snippets
must_fix.issue: The dossier's 'verified' Discord snippets use `DiscordIpcClient::new("<id>")?` and were run against discord-rich-presence 0.2, where new() returned Result. In the pinned 1.1.0, new() returns Self (verified: ipc_unix.rs:43 `pub fn new<T: AsRef<str>>(client_id: T) -> Self`), so the `?` will not compile. The public error type is also now discord_rich_presence::error::Error (a thiserror enum), not Box<dyn Error>. An agent copying the dossier idiom verbatim writes non-compiling code.
must_fix.concrete_edit: Add a one-line pitfall note in design.md §2 (discord row) or tasks.md 2.2: 'discord-rich-presence 1.1.0: DiscordIpcClient::new(id) returns Self (no `?`); connect/reconnect/set_activity/clear_activity/close return Result<(), discord_rich_presence::error::Error>; the dossier C1/C2 snippets are 0.2-era and must drop the `?` on new().' Add an AC to tasks.md 2.2 requiring the sink to compile against the pinned 1.1.0.
must_fix.raised_by: R2, R4 (verified against crate 1.1.0 source on this Mac)

must_fix.location: requirements.md Product goal line 19 + FR-5/AC-2 line 99; design.md §4.3 party.size row line 131; tasks.md 2.1 line 81
must_fix.issue: party.size is defined four inconsistent ways: product goal line 19 says element0 = sessions 'running' (live count); FR-5/AC-2 says [busy_count, total_live_count]; design §4.3 says [busy_count, max] OR [busy_count, total_count]; tasks 2.1 says [busy,total/capacity]. The '(2 of 5)' example is non-derivable, and making element0 = busy_count means the counter shows '(0 of N)' or risks disappearing whenever all sessions are idle (no tool mid-run) — the common state. NOTE: the claim that Discord OMITS the party when current=0 is community lore, NOT confirmed in the official docs.discord.food schema (which only documents [current,max] with no zero-handling), so the rationale is 'avoid a degenerate/empty counter', not a documented hard rule.
must_fix.concrete_edit: Pick ONE definition and apply it in all four places. Recommended: `party.size = [live_count, capacity]`, where live_count = total live sessions and capacity = configured max (FR-7/AC-1), defaulting to live_count when capacity is unset. This makes element0 ≥ 1 whenever any session exists and matches the product-goal '(N of M) running' intent. Convey busy/idle via state text or small_image, not party.current. Rewrite requirements.md:19 and FR-5/AC-2:99, collapse design §4.3:131 to the single form, and update tasks.md 2.1:81.
must_fix.raised_by: R1, R2, R8 (cross-doc contradiction verified; the 'current=0 hides party' mechanism is community-sourced, not in official docs)

should_fix.issue: The spec specifies ZERO plist keys for a lifecycle-critical artifact, and leaves KeepAlive vs graceful-shutdown unresolved. Bare KeepAlive=true (per `man launchd.plist`: 'unconditionally keep the job alive') relaunches the daemon right after the exit-0 that cleared the presence, re-setting it within ~1s and racing `uninstall` bootout — directly breaking FR-8/AC-3.
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add a concrete plist key list to design §3 / tasks 4.1: Label com.<author>.claude-presence, ProgramArguments=[<absolute binary path>, 'run'], RunAtLoad=true, KeepAlive={SuccessfulExit:false} (restart only on crash), StandardOutPath/StandardErrorPath under the log dir, ProcessType=Background. Do NOT set EnvironmentVariables.TMPDIR/HOME (gui/<uid> agents inherit per-user values; hardcoding the dynamic /var/folders TMPDIR is a bug). Add an FR-8 AC: uninstall must `launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)` BEFORE the process exits so it cannot relaunch after clearing presence.

should_fix.location: tasks.md 1.4 (Files: src/claude/activity.rs) and 1.5 (Files: src/claude/transcript.rs) vs 2.1 (Files: src/state/model.rs); design.md §4.2 lines 111-116
should_fix.issue: SessionState and Activity are defined in src/state/model.rs, owned exclusively by task 2.1. But task 1.4 produces Activity and task 1.5 emits SessionState, and 2.1 Depends on 1.5 — so these shared types have no owning task before their consumers, and a fresh agent on 1.5 cannot compile against a type that doesn't exist yet and isn't in its Files.
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add a new early task `0.3 Core domain types` (Files: src/state/model.rs defining SessionState, Activity, and the PresenceModel skeleton; Depends: 0.1). Add 0.3 to the Depends of tasks 1.4, 1.5, and 2.1, and remove src/state/model.rs from task 2.1's Files (2.1 keeps only src/state/aggregator.rs).

should_fix.location: requirements.md FR-2/AC-2 (line 58); tasks.md 1.4 (line 63); design.md §3 privacy.rs (line 63) and §4.3 details row
should_fix.issue: The Bash activity becomes the PUBLIC `details` string 'Running command <cmd>' built from the raw tool_input.command, but the only sanitizers with an AC are paths→basename + blacklist. A command like `curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer sk-...'`, `export GH_TOKEN=...`, or a credentialed URL flows verbatim onto the public profile. 'strip secrets' appears only as an unbacked aside in design.md:63.
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add an AC to FR-2/AC-2 and a sub-step to tasks 1.4: for Bash, by default derive the verb from only the program/first token ('Running cargo', 'Running git') and DROP arguments; behind config, optionally show a scrubbed command (strip token/key/secret/password/Authorization patterns, WORD=value env-assignments, credentialed URLs, and long base64/hex blobs) then truncate. Add a test in tasks 5.1 asserting a command containing a fake token never appears in `details`.

should_fix.location: requirements.md FR-8 / NFR-3; design.md §3 logging (line 49); tasks.md 3.1, 4.1
should_fix.issue: No permission/log-content requirements: ~/.config and ~/.local/state are 0755 by default (verified live), nothing forbids `tracing::debug!(?event)` writing the raw tool_input.command or full statusline JSON to a (possibly 0644) log file, and the ingest socket has no permission or peer-uid check (any local process could spoof presence).
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add to FR-7/FR-8: create the state and config dirs 0700; write config.toml and log files 0600; bind daemon.sock 0600 inside the 0700 state dir and have the ingest server verify peer uid == own uid (getpeereid). Add an NFR-3/FR-8 AC: the daemon MUST NOT log raw tool_input, prompt/transcript text, full paths, or unredacted statusline JSON at any level — only redacted summaries; apply the same sanitizers used for Discord output to logs. Add a privacy.rs test in tasks 5.1 that the IngestEvent log formatter omits command/paths.

should_fix.issue: The `forward` subcommand is required by design ADR-9 and wired in tasks 0.1/3.1/3.2/3.3, but is absent from requirements FR-8/AC-2's subcommand list AND from the design §2 clap row — yet task 0.1 cites FR-8/AC-2 as the AC authorizing it. The AC the task points to doesn't actually list `forward`.
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add `forward` to requirements.md FR-8/AC-2 (e.g. '...`doctor`, and an internal `forward` used by the chained hook/statusline scripts to pipe events to the daemon socket; not user-facing') and to the design.md §2 CLI table row, so all three docs agree.

should_fix.location: design.md §2 deps table (lines 40-51), §4.3 buttons row (line 134), §7 Risks; requirements.md FR-7/AC-2; tasks.md 0.1 deps line, 5.3
should_fix.issue: Two unaddressed button facts: (a) buttons set over local IPC are NOT visible on your OWN profile (verified via Discord docs/community) — defeating this self-viewing tool's whole point; (b) Discord requires https:// button URLs, yet the dossier's mapping uses buttons[0]=file:///Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private (an invalid URL AND a full-home-path + private-repo-URL privacy leak). Buttons are also not in the classic SET_ACTIVITY schema. Separately, thiserror is mandated by CLAUDE.md but missing from the deps table and tasks 0.1, and notify is unpinned (latest is 9.0.0-rc.4 prerelease; stable is 8.2.0).
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add a §7 risk row noting buttons may not render on the user's own profile via local IPC → keep buttons OFF by default and opt-in per repo, never emit a file:// URL or a private-repo remote URL, and add a doctor note. Annotate the §4.3 buttons row accordingly. Add an FR-7/AC-2 AC for the buttons privacy/https rule. Add a `thiserror` row to design §2 and to tasks 0.1's dep list. Pin notify as `notify = "8"` in design §2 and tasks 0.1.

should_fix.issue: Stop (and other events) do not support a matcher (silently ignored). The live Stop hook is [{matcher:'', hooks:[{command:'afplay .../Submarine.aiff'}]}]. Chaining must append our forwarder into the existing group's hooks[] and on uninstall remove only our exact command object by identity, never key on matcher equality, or it drops/duplicates the afplay hook. Also: if hooks[<Event>] doesn't exist on a fresh user, install must create it. And no drift rule exists for the realistic case where the user edits statusLine.command or hooks after install (the live statusline is a multi-line jq string users keep tweaking).
should_fix.concrete_edit: In tasks 3.3 / ADR-4 specify: read existing hooks array; if hooks[<Event>] absent, create it; append {type:command, command:'claude-presence forward --kind hook'} into the existing group's hooks[]; on uninstall remove only our exact command object by identity. Add an AC: at install record both the original and the wrapped value; at uninstall restore the original only if the current value still equals our installed wrapper, else surgically remove only our segment/entry and warn. Add a fixture test using the real afplay Stop entry.

should_fix.location: design.md §1 architecture diagram (lines 13-15) and the notify '$TMPDIR for discord socket creation' note
should_fix.issue: The §1 diagram labels collectors `collectors::sessions/transcript/ingest`, implying a src/collectors/ module that does not exist — §3 and every tasks.md Files entry use src/claude/* and src/ingest/*. Separately, the diagram implies notify watches $TMPDIR for discord-ipc-N socket creation, but the sink design (FR-6/AC-1) just probes ipc-0..9 with backoff; no task implements a $TMPDIR watch, and FSEvents coalescing makes it an unreliable 'Discord started' trigger.
should_fix.concrete_edit: In design.md §1 relabel the diagram nodes to the real module paths (claude::sessions, claude::transcript, ingest::socket) or annotate that collectors:: is a conceptual grouping. Drop the '$TMPDIR for discord socket creation' phrase from the notify description (keep notify scoped to transcript/subagent watching, which is what tasks 1.5 implements).

should_fix.issue: sysinfo accessors are listed as bare calls (exe()/cwd()/cpu_usage()/start_time()), but on sysinfo 0.30+ (pinned 0.39.3) cwd/exe/cmd are NOT populated by default (verified via docs.rs: they require with_cwd/with_exe(UpdateKind)). An agent doing a default refresh gets cwd()==None for every process and silently falls through to libproc on 100% of sessions.
should_fix.concrete_edit: In design.md §2 and tasks.md 1.3 note the refresh requirement: refresh with ProcessRefreshKind::nothing().with_exe(UpdateKind::Always).with_cwd(UpdateKind::Always).with_cpu() over ProcessesToUpdate::All before reading exe()/cwd(). Also note cpu_usage() needs two refreshes spaced by MINIMUM_CPU_UPDATE_INTERVAL to be non-zero.

should_fix.issue: ai-title is extracted (FR-2/AC-3) and the dossier proposes it for large_text/idle display, but no requirement gates it through redaction or the blacklist. It is model-generated from the user's prompt (e.g. 'Analyze Claude rich presence implementation feasibility') and would render the work topic on the public profile.
should_fix.concrete_edit: Add to FR-7/AC-2 (and tasks 5.1): ai-title is OFF by default; only shown when a config flag is set AND the project is not blacklisted, and must pass the same redaction path as `details`. Add a note that extraction != emission — route ai-title through privacy.rs before any Discord field.

should_fix.location: tasks.md 1.1 AC list (line 36) vs 3.1 AC list (line 109)
should_fix.issue: Task 1.1 claims FR-3/AC-2 (extract cost/ctx%/model FROM statusLine JSON), but 1.1 only builds the serde struct/parser; the actual extraction-into-the-model is done by task 3.1 (ingest/override), which does NOT list FR-3/AC-2. The extraction AC is traced to a task that doesn't perform it. (Minor — 1.1 legitimately owns the field PARSER.)

should_fix.issue: Conflicting truncation priority: FR-5/AC-3 says 'abbreviate model/plan first, drop least-important metric last' (abbreviate, incl. plan, precedes any drop); design §5 says 'drop metrics from the tail (ctx% → tokens → cost → plan) and abbreviate model' (drop first, and treats plan as a droppable metric). plan is abbreviate-first in one, drop-last in the other.
should_fix.concrete_edit: State one ordered algorithm in both: e.g. (1) abbreviate model 'Opus 4.8'→'O4.8'; (2) abbreviate plan; (3) if still >128, drop metrics from the tail ctx% → tokens → cost. Mirror the identical ladder in requirements.md FR-5/AC-3 and design §5.

nice_to_have: Add a single-instance guard (FR-8 AC): a launchd agent plus an accidental foreground `claude-presence run` produce two writers to discord-ipc-N, exceeding the 5/20s rate limit and blanking the presence. On startup acquire an exclusive lock (flock on the state dir or socket ownership); if another live instance is detected, exit clearly and have doctor report the conflict.

Add an empty-state AC (FR-5): when zero live sessions exist, clear the Discord presence (activity:null) and hold cleared until a session appears (configurable: clear vs generic idle card). This also makes FR-5/AC-1 focus selection well-defined for the no-session case.
Add an explicit Discord-not-running-at-startup AC (extend FR-6/AC-1 or AC-4): if the initial probe of discord-ipc-0..9 finds zero sockets, retry with backoff rather than exiting.

Replace tilde timing values with concrete defaults already in design (min_interval=2.5s, keepalive=15s) and give the <10ms hook-handler AC a measurement basis + tolerance (e.g. <10ms p95 over 100 invocations, relax to the documented <50ms hook budget if unattainable).
Add an integration/e2e smoke test task (tests/e2e.rs, Depends 2.3) feeding fixture sessions through the aggregator and asserting a non-empty PresenceModel, since only unit tests exist today.

Add owning tasks for the thiserror error enum (src/error.rs) and tracing-subscriber init incl. the rotating file appender (src/logging.rs), and make 2.3/4.1 depend on them; also assign git-remote reading (for buttons) to a concrete task/module instead of leaving it implied in design §4.3.
Standardize launchctl terminology on bootstrap/bootout gui/$(id -u) in both design.md:79 (currently 'load/unload') and tasks.md (currently 'uninstall unloads').
Note FR-4/AC-1 lists the MVP-minimum hook subset; SessionEnd and CwdChanged exist in the product (confirmed in the binary) and are optional adds for cleanup/project-switching.
confirmed_strengths: Every Anthropic identifier the specs depend on is confirmed present in the live v2.1.181 binary: hook events SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/SubagentStart/SubagentStop, and statusLine fields total_cost_usd, used_percentage, context_window_size, display_name, total_duration_ms, exceeds_200k_tokens, effort.

The chosen crate discord-rich-presence 1.1.0 (vionya) genuinely exists, is the latest stable, and its builder exposes every field the card maps (state, details, Timestamps::start(i64), Assets::{large/small_image,_text}, Party::size([i32;2]), Button::new), plus connect/reconnect/set_activity/clear_activity/close — verified in the cached crate source on this Mac.
The Opus 4.8 = 1M context window (not 200k) decision is correct and consistently applied (CLAUDE.md, FR-3/AC-4, tasks 1.2).

The exe-path process filter (avoiding pgrep -f truncation), the sessions/<PID>.json + kill(pid,0) liveness model, and focus = most-recently-active (avoiding Accessibility prompts) are all sound and grounded in the dossier.

The statusLine setting shape and stdin-JSON delivery, the chain-install-never-overwrite principle, the 128-char caps, and the 5/20s rate-limit handling (debounce + coalesce + keepalive) are all accurate vs official docs.

The declared task Depends graph is acyclic and AC traceability is nearly complete — every task AC reference resolves to a real AC with no orphans (the only defects are the 2.3/3.1 main.rs collision and the type-ownership inversion).
contradictions_or_disagreements: party.size element-0 semantics: R1 recommends busy_count as element 0; R2 argues against it (busy=0 risks the counter disappearing) and recommends live/total. My check: the official docs.discord.food schema documents only [current,max] and does NOT confirm 'current=0 omits the party' — that is community lore, not an official rule. The real, verifiable defect is the four-way cross-doc contradiction; I sided with R2's [live_count, capacity] because it resolves the contradiction AND keeps element0 >= 1 whenever sessions exist, matching the product-goal 'running' intent. Flagged the omission rationale as community-sourced in the must_fix.

R3 mislabeled two findings as 'blocking' that are not: (a) its 'exceeds_200k_tokens nesting' finding self-concludes 'Spec is already correct. No change needed' — a non-finding; verified exceeds_200k_tokens is top-level and the spec treats it correctly. (b) R3's statusline-wrapper finding IS a real major but its proposed CC_PRESENCE_INNER-as-env fix is wrong (CC cannot inject env vars) — the correct fix is baking the stored command into the wrapper, which R6/R8 got right.

R3's hook-event 'incompleteness' (missing SessionEnd/CwdChanged) is a nit, not a gap: the 6 chosen events all exist and are sufficient for MVP activity tracking. No reviewer treated it as blocking; I kept it nice_to_have.

R4's 'notify watches $TMPDIR for socket creation' is a doc-narrative inconsistency, not a code issue — no task implements such a watch, so it is should_fix (clean up the diagram), not a build blocker. R4 and R2 both independently and correctly caught the new()-returns-Self compile issue (high agreement).
